observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Inventory Committee:
Conduct a complete physical count of all its properties, prepare and update the Property Cards, and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for all types and per class of PPE; and
Use the appropriate forms and reports prescribed under the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs Volume 2.
We also recommended and the management agreed that the Acting Municipal Accountant prepare the PPE Ledger Cards for all PPEs starting from the most recent acquisitions and working backward until all PPEs are with PPE Ledger Cards, continuously update the same, and regularly reconciled with the inventory report of the Inventory Committee.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Acting Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the costs of completed projects in the appropriate asset accounts and to ensure that the Construction in Progress account balances reflect only the accumulated costs for projects that are still in the process of development or construction.

We recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the following:
All the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances in strict compliance with the existing rules and regulations and to observe the prescribed liquidation periods for all cash advances; and
The Municipal Accountant to:
Issue within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30 days per COA Circular 2012-001 or one calendar month period specified under Section 22 of Executive Order No. 77 for official local travel, a written reminder signed by the head of agency or his authorized representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to submit an accounting of the cash advance received for his/her travel, following the applicable rules and regulation;
Issue demand letters to those accountable officers with outstanding cash advances that are already beyond the liquidation period;
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her has been fully liquidated or a proper accounting thereof is made; and
Strictly ensure that cash advances granted to accountable officers are within their maximum bonded cash accountability.

We recommended and it was agreed that the LCE direct:
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and provide a copy of the list of unreleased checks to the Municipal Accountant at the end of the year; and
The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments for unreleased checks at year-end, to present fairly the Cash in Bank and corresponding Payable accounts in the Financial Statements (FS).

We recommended and it was agreed that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to exhaust efforts to retrieve the supporting documents, reconcile the discrepancy between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data in the FS, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended and it was agreed that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to present the actual balance of the prepaid insurance in order to come up with a more accurate presentation of the Financial Statements.

We recommended and it was agreed that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to recognize the cost of the equipment procured in the appropriate asset account.

Based on the foregoing audit observations, we recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The HRMO:
To regularly update the leave cards of each official and employee and provide the Acting Municipal Accountant with the necessary data for the computation of the cost of employee benefits, in preparation for the full implementation of COA Circular No. 2024-009 and its quarterly update thereafter; and
To furnish each department/office with an updated schedule of leave credits earned for their respective officials and employees every month or as frequently as practicable.
The heads of departments/offices to include in their respective budget proposals for the succeeding fiscal year the monetary value of outstanding leave credits and the estimated cost of employee benefits which could be earned during the succeeding years.

We recommended and it was agreed that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments and comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by following the prescribed accounting entries in order to come up with a more accurate presentation of the Financial Statements.

We recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Accountant and concerned LGU personnel to submit the lacking documents such as the required project proposals with breakdown of expenses, certifications, liquidation reports, distribution lists, receipts and those documents identified in the Annex, within 15 calendar days from receipt of this report.

We recommended and it was agreed that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant and concerned LGU personnel to submit the lacking documents, such as the required certificates of attendance, invitations, itineraries of travel, and certificates of travel completed among others, within 15 calendar days from receipt of this report.
We also recommended and it was agreed, that the LCE cause the concerned LGU personnel to refund the excess travel allowances claimed.

We recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Acting Municipal Accountant and concerned LGU personnel to submit the lacking documents mentioned above such as the Capacity Development Agenda, detailed activity design, original attendance sheets, certificates of appearance/participation to seminars/trainings and those documents identified in Annex H, within 15 calendar days from receipt of this report to the audit team.

We recommended and it was agreed that the LCE direct the MDRRMO, Acting Municipal Accountant, and other department heads concerned to discontinue charging the cost of daily operations of the Radio Station and other inappropriate charges to the LDRRMF.

We recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) immediately notify the Bureau of the Treasury regarding the bondable job-order personnel and initiate the application for their respective fidelity bonds; otherwise, these personnel should not be assigned bondable tasks.
We recommended and it was agreed that the Management inform all LGU personnel of the minimum net take home pay requirement and strictly ensure compliance with the GAA provisions.

We recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure the regular and prompt preparation of the computation of the RPT shares of each barangay and comply with the requirement of RA No. 7160 to release the same to the Barangays within 5 days from the end of each quarter.

We recommended and it was agreed that the GFPS conduct gender analysis and/or gender assessment using sex-disaggregated data, and accordingly revise the Municipality’s GAD Agenda as required under Item C2 par. 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to establish a clear strategic framework for gender mainstreaming, women empowerment, and gender equality; and ensure that annual GPB is prepared in accordance with the GAD Agenda so that programs and projects are responsive to the  GAD issues actually identified and aligned with the Municipality’s GAD goals.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the following:
Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs, prepare and timely submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team; and
The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office - Designate reconcile and update their respective PPE records, and make necessary adjustments for noted discrepancies.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the LCE create a special committee to handle the procedural requirements on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided for under the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to do the following:
Discontinue the practice of recording the Cash Advances as Accounts Payables since there are still no obligations incurred and to strictly adhere to paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
Henceforth, record the unpaid salaries, benefits and other obligations due to officials and employees of the Municipality under the Due to Officers and Employees account.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct all the officers and employees to expedite the processing of the DVs and other documents needed for the issuance of checks and to submit them to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office on or before the deadline set to avoid the recurrence of the same issues/concerns.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to exhaust efforts to retrieve the supporting documents, reconcile the discrepancy of ₱1.525 million between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of PD 
No. 1445 and paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

Thus, we recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to review the entries made to the asset accounts and ensure that necessary adjustments are made and henceforth, observe the recording of transactions to appropriate accounts for fair presentation.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-002 by:
Including in the notes to the Financial Statements the required minimum disclosures on LDRRMF as presented in Annex D of the Circular;
Reviewing the current procedure in accounting for the transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF and make necessary adjustments; and
Reviewing the current practice of recording only the 5% of the NTA received monthly as funds available for LDRRMF and make necessary adjustments to include the 5% from other regular sources.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to do the following:
Submit to the audit team the lacking documents mentioned above as well as those documents identified in the attached Annexes within 15 calendar days from receipt of this report; and
Take note of the documentary requirements for common government transactions as provided for in COA Circular 
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and ensure completeness of supporting documents of paid disbursement vouchers prior to submission to the audit team.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) together with the members of the Local Peace and Order Council and other responsible officials and employees to:
See to it that the specific allocation for Peace and Order Program is included in the Municipality’s Annual Budget and Annual Investment Plan for the year;
Make sure that the appropriation for Confidential Funds will be included in the budget for POP; and
Ensure that the total amount allocated for Confidential Fund shall not exceed 30 percent of the total annual amount allocated for the LGU’s POP.
We also recommended and the Management agreed to discontinue the practice of utilizing the Cash Advance for Confidential Fund to pay/reimburse expenses made prior to the granting of the cash advance.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LDRRMFIP be prepared accordingly to include all programs and activities to be implemented for the year and submit the approved plan to the RDRRMC as required.
We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE assign responsible personnel to coordinate with the OP regarding the said fund transfer to the Municipality of Carles for its refund.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS prepare the Municipality’s GAD Agenda for the three-year period as required under Item C2 par. 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to establish the Municipality’s strategic framework and plan on gender mainstreaming, women empowerment and gender equality; and prepare the annual GPB in accordance with the GAD Agenda to ensure that identified programs and projects will answer GAD issues that directly relates to the GAD goals of the Municipality for the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), and the concerned officials/personnel and the BAC do the following:
Submit explanation duly supported with documents to justify the actions taken on the aforementioned transactions and for further evaluation by the audit team in order to avoid audit suspensions or disallowances; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR on all procurement activities of the LGU in order to avoid future audit suspensions or disallowances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE designate an official/employee to be responsible for consolidating the data from each department and prepare and submit the quarterly Report on Government PPA to the audit team within the prescribed period.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct all department heads concerned to prepare the list of all ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPAs and submit it to the designated official/employee for consolidation.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The LCE designate personnel who will consolidate the detailed action plans of each department and at the same time will prepare and submit the AAPSI to the audit team;
The LCE direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2022 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years to the personnel assigned to consolidate the same; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Government, within sixty (60) days from its receipt of the Annual Audit Report, submit to the audit team, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations therein.
Creation of an Appraisal Committee to aid in the conduct of the physical count of all its properties, especially those which were acquired in prior years; and

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget
   Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  MDRRM  Officer,

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  concerned

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:


   1. GFPS headed by the GAD Focal Person and  all  other  Department  Heads
      concerned to:

     a. Ensure that  all  members  of  the  GFPS  including  elected  local
        officials and department heads undergo capacity  building  programs
        on GAD;




     b. Set up appropriate systems and mechanisms to ensure the generation,
        processing, review, and updating of sex-disaggregated data  or  GAD
        database  to  serve  as  basis  in  performance-based  and  gender-
        responsive planning and budgeting;


     c. Give priority to those  PPAs  that  will  address  emerging  and/or
        continuing issues and concerns at all times;




     d. Use the HGDG tool for GAD fund  attribution.  If  the  LGU  is  not
        trained on the HGDG, it may propose a training program on the  HGDG
        in its GPB for the incoming year and may seek the assistance  of  a
        gender  analysis   specialist/expert   in   the   use   of   and/or
        administration of the HGDG;




     e. Seek technical assistance from the DILG on the formulation  of  LGU
        GPBs and on the application of the HGDG;




     f. Ensure that PPAs implemented are  strictly  included  in  the  DILG
        endorsed GPB or enacted LGU budget;




     g. Properly fill out the  GPB  and  GAD  AR  in  accordance  with  the

     h. Avoid charging to GAD fund expenses that do  not  directly  address
        gender issues.




   1. MPDO to set up and maintain GAD database to serve as basis for gender-
      responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;


   2. GAD GFPS or TWG  to  provide  technical  assistance  to  the  MPDO  in
      ensuring that the GAD Database is operational;


   3. Reconstitution of GAD Committee and Technical Working  Group  for  the
      GFPS; and


   4. Acting Municipal Accountant to submit all other Disbursement  Vouchers
      and  supporting  documents  relative   to   GAD   PPAs   for   further
      verification.

We recommended that the LCE require the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each existing stallholder for monitoring and record-keeping purposes; and

Make the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the amount receivable from the market stallholders at year end.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Agriculturist that the amount released for the livelihood assistance amounting to ₱100,000.00 will be refunded. Henceforth, stop the inclusion or implementation of projects that appears to have a conflict of interest.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

Concerned personnel to submit liquidation report of cash advances mentioned with relevant supporting papers, lacking documents and explanation on audit observations noted;

Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that only eligible suppliers are negotiated with and relevant rules and regulations be complied; and

OIC-Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRMO to review the completeness and ascertain the veracity of the documents attached to the disbursement vouchers before payment is made.
We recommended that the LCE designate a focal person responsible for assessing the costs to be incurred in titling of lots and the inclusion of costs related thereto in the Municipality’s budget.

We recommended that the LCE require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

Execute lease contracts with market stallholders to enhance enforceability of the ordinance and ensure that all the applicable terms and conditions were reflected therein; and

Provide the Acting Municipal Accountant data e.g., list of market occupants, corresponding rental rate, the amount due from them as at December 31, 2020 and payments made thereafter.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MDRRM Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and Other Persons Responsible to:

Draft a budgetary proposal for the appropriation of MOOE and Capital Outlay of the MDRRM Office; and

Avoid charging any MOOE and Capital Outlay of the MDRRMO to the 5% MDRRM Fund. Non-compliance with the pertinent provisions of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 may lead to issuance of audit suspensions/disallowances.
We recommended that the LCE direct the MDRRMO to exert effort in preparing the monthly Report on Utilization of the DRRMF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor promptly.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GAD Focal Person, members of the GFPS and concerned personnel to:

1. Submit the final GPB and its corresponding GAD AR for CY 2020 to the Office of the Auditor as soon as possible for the assessment of implemented GAD PPAs;

2. Submit the Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documents of transactions charged under the GAD Fund for audit in order to determine its propriety, validity and legality; and

3. Henceforth, follow the schedule prescribed by the PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM JMC 2013-01 and JMC 2016-1 in GAD planning and budgeting and include programs/projects/activities that are GAD related/directly addresses gender issue.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:

1. Concerned Department Heads to:

• Review the status of implementation of the Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) and submit the corresponding liquidation report of those fully implemented PPAs to the concerned National Government Agencies in order to settle the obligations of the Municipal Government; • Submit Accomplishment Report or Status of all PPAs being implemented by the Municipal Government to the Office of the Auditor; and • Implement, as soon as possible, those projects whose funds were already transferred to the Municipal Government’s Bank Account for more than one year and not fully executed or carried out as of December 31, 2019 for the benefit of the residents/constituents of the Municipality.

2. Acting Municipal Accountant to analyze the breakdown of the accounts presented in the Trial Balance, gather relevant documents in order to establish valid obligations, provide the necessary subsidiary ledgers to support the balances and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted, to clean up the Due to NGAs Account.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

1. Compute the borrowing cost to be capitalized as at year-end according to the Standard and recognize it as part of the cost of the qualifying asset by making a correcting entry; and

2. Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements (a) the accounting policy adopted for borrowing costs; (b) the amount of borrowing costs capitalized during the period; and (c) the capitalization rate used to determine the amount of borrowing costs eligible for capitalization (when it was necessary to apply a capitalization rate to funds borrowed generally) as prescribed by Paragraph 40 of PPSAS 5.

We recommended that the LCE direct:

1. Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected expense accounts and henceforth, use appropriate accounts in recording transactions; and

2. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that there is an appropriate budget for training and travelling expenses before certifying as to the existence of available appropriation pursuant to Section 4 (1) of PD No. 1445, which states that no money shall be paid out of any public treasury or depository except in pursuance of an appropriation law or other specific statutory authority, in order to avoid future audit suspensions/disallowances.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

1. Human Resource and Management Officer:

• Furnish every Office the updated schedule of leave credits earned of the Officials/Employees concerned. Earned vacation and sick leave shall be presented separately. The data can be used in drafting budget proposals relative to the monetization of leave credits earned, payment of terminal leave and for easy verification if the information presented therein were accurate or not; and

• Ensure that Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave were followed before certifying in the Application for Leave on every request for commutation of leave credits.

2. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that payments of leave credits earned shall not exceed the approved appropriation in order to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.

3. The Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entry in order to reflect the correct balances of accounts involved.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the following:

Concerned officials and employees to adhere strictly and comply with the requirements of Section 5.14 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and as prescribed in Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292; and total payments on monetization for the entire year shall not exceed its approved budget contrary to Section 4(1) of PD No. 1445, thus validity and necessity of the claims could not be ascertained and resulted to irregular disbursements.

The Acting Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents before payment; and HRMO to be thorough in monitoring the leave credit balances and preparing the certification of leave balances and other documents needed in payment of related claims.

We recommended that the LCE direct:

1. The Municipal Budget Officer to:

• Submit as soon as possible the revised computation for Personal Services (PS) limitation with complete details and information;

• Make sure the existence of available appropriation before certifying in the Obligation Request in order to avoid audit suspension/disallowances; and

• See to it that appropriations for PS expenses will be within the limitation prescribed by Section 325 (a) of RA No. 7160.

2. The Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements for incentives/bonuses/allowances are in accordance with the established rules and regulations before payment; and

3. The Officials and Employees concerned to return, as soon as possible, the incentives/bonuses/allowances received in excess of the PS Cap limitation in order to avoid issuance of audit suspension/disallowances.

We recommended that the LCE direct:

1. The Personnel Rewards and Recognition Committee to:

• Establish an employee and incentive rewards system or PRAISE and shall adhere to the rules and regulations prescribed by CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01, s. 2001; and

• Make sure that monetary awards shall be granted only when the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal effort of the nominee resulted in monetary savings which shall not exceed 20 percent of the savings generated; and

• Submit the PRAISE and its amendments, if any, to the Civil Service Regional Office, copy furnished please the Office of the Audit Team Leader.

2. The Human Resource Management Officer to provide a copy of the job performance evaluation and assessment results showing if indeed those awardees have suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts which contribute to the efficiency, economy, or other improvement in government operations, or other extraordinary acts or services in the public interest.

3. The Acting Municipal Accountant to reclassify the expenses as Personal Services based on its nature and make sure, henceforth, that the supporting documents for the Disbursement Vouchers are complete before payment; and

4. The Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that there is indeed an existence of available appropriation for a particular disbursement and the same are in accordance with the nature of the account before certifying the Obligation Request.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned personnel to:

1. Require the suppliers – SBT Store and CFCMPC – to submit the BIR-registered Charge Invoices and Delivery Receipts, and properly fill out the forms as prescribed by the NIRC. Present the original copy of the Official Receipt issued by the CFCMC; and

2. Make sure that the procurement documents e.g., canvass forms and Purchase Orders are properly and completely filled out and that the suppliers are accredited. Section 53 of the Revised IRR for 2016 of RA No. 9184 states in part that Negotiated Procurement is a method of procurement of Goods, Infrastructure Projects and Consulting services, whereby the Procuring Entity negotiates a contract with a technically, legally and financially capable supplier, contractor or consultant.

We recommended that the LCE direct the person in-charge to submit promptly the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor for easy audit verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2019 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices and the information gathered therein shall be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor as soon as possible.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Government, shall within sixty (60) days from its receipt of the Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant to make necessary actions to enforce collection of the funds due to the government; Concerned accountable officers to remit all collections on hand and the liquidating officer to deposit the same not later than the next banking day; and Concerned accountable officers to remit collections and ensure deposit daily or in the next banking day.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant to immediately revert back to cash and set-up the corresponding liability account/s of the stale checks identified using the guidelines prescribed under Item 4 b and c, Section 83 (b) of the DOF-BLGF Local Operations Manual; Acting Municipal Accountant to verify with the AGDB of the Municipality the validity of the subject credit memo, and immediately effect any adjustments in the books of accounts, if warranted; Acting Municipal Accountant to work hand in hand with the OIC-Municipal Treasurer in tracing the records of the transactions relative to the ₱32,000.00 deposit credited by the AGDB of the Municipality to its bank account but not recorded by the latter; Municipal Treasurer to request for snapshot bank statements for every transaction with the AGDB of the Municipality to facilitate prompt reconciliation of book and bank balances; Municipal Treasurer to verify with the AGDB the veracity of the deposits made amounting to ₱30,000.00 which were recorded in the books but not credited by the bank; If found void, the OIC-Municipal Treasurer shall charge the floating amount to the responsible Accountable Officer who declared such deposit/s.

We recommended that the LCE direct the responsible personnel to:

Conduct feasibility study as to the availability of water source since it is the most vital of all the processes in the realization of the project;

Impose liquidated damages against the erring Contractor in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR, if warranted; and

Comply with the responsibilities of the Municipal Government, as required by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-143, particularly on the organization of the Water Sanitation (WATSAN) Council/Committee and Poor Communities with no Access to Safe Drinking Water; and strengthen its monitoring functions, among others.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to formulate strategies in order to efficiently and effectively implement all the projects funded under the Municipality’s continuing appropriations and ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF in compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and MPDC to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the projects under the 20% DF, and if the same is in accordance with the approved development plan and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program as prescribed in Section 2.2 of the above JMC.

We recommended that the LCE direct the full implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003, together with the members of the SWM Board, in order to avoid administrative sanctions stated in Section 50 of the Act; Require the SWM Task Force to impose the fees, fines and penalties due to the LGU as provided in Section 47 and 49 of the same Act; and Eventually require the establishment of a sanitary landfill which is the ultimate requirement in ensuring the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality as prescribed by RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to examine the books of accounts and pertinent records of the businesses once every tax period and comply with the manner of examination as prescribed by the Manual.

We recommended that the LCE direct:

The persons responsible to submit to the Office of the Auditor the following: List of positions in the plantilla with the corresponding job description/actual duties that describe the basis for justifying that such positions are exposed to occupational risks and assigned to places stated in Item 16.1.1 of the abovementioned IRR; Written procedure in identifying the personnel who are entitled to hazard pay as prescribed under item 16.1.2.2 of the IRR; and

If none was prepared, provisions in Items 16.1.2.1 and 16.1.2.2 of the IRR shall be complied. Strict compliance of the above-cited provisions by providing complete supporting documents for future claims to establish its validity and in order to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.

The Municipal Accountant to check the accuracy of the claim and completeness of the required supporting documents before payment; The persons concerned, who have availed of the hazard pay, to submit the duly approved DTRs, Travel Reports and Individual Accomplishment Reports to the Office of the Auditor for further evaluation of the claim. Otherwise, audit suspensions/disallowances may be issued, if warranted; and The persons concerned to refund the excess claim of hazard pay, if any.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:

Creation of policies and procedures on the payment for repairs and maintenance of property and equipment of the Municipality, be it regular, preventive, and corrective maintenance or emergency repairs, and observe the limitations prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-003;

General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to properly evaluate the cost of repair and maintenance of the equipment owned by the Municipality, and the General Services Officer to maintain Waste Materials Report for the parts to be replaced; and

Concerned officers and employees to undergo proper procurement processes in accordance with RA No. 9184, for repairs and maintenance of property and equipment.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:

Demand from the collectors the prompt turnover of collections at the close of each business day;

Request the collectors assigned in the field to remit to the Designated Liquidating Officer the collections at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00; and

Remind the Liquidating Officer to: Make sure that the Accountable Officers observe issuance of pre-numbered official receipts in strict numerical sequence and check that cancelled Accountable Forms are attached in the Report of Collections before submission to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and eventually to the Office of the Auditor for audit; Double check the correctness of the collections being remitted to avoid under/over remittance and sign the Reports accordingly; and Deposit intact all collections turned over by the collectors with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to include a discussion on the details of the unexpended balances of the MDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements as required in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

MDRRM Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the records on hand and present the correct balance of the unutilized MDRRM Fund in the Report on the Utilization of DRRM Fund and Notes to the FS and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Department Heads to include the projects, under the ADM Program, in the APP of the ensuing year considering that the projects are still ongoing; The persons responsible to re-assess the project schedule/timeline and the actual implementation of the project and impose liquidated damages, if warranted; The persons responsible to attach the complete bidding documents to facilitate the timely audit of disbursements; The Engineering Department to attach related pictures before, during and after the construction, detailed drawings/plans and specifications and other necessary documents. The said documents will be used by the Audit Team in conducting inspection of the implemented projects.

We recommended that the LCE inform the Office of the SA and ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, and direct the concerned Department Heads to include in the Report of GPPAs, all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; Concerned Department Heads to maintain as current as possible the Project Status as mentioned in Section 3.2 of the Circular; and Assignment of a focal person that will consolidate/update the report and monitor the progress of the PPAs every reporting period and submit promptly to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the reports and supporting documents of the months that are already recorded in the books of accounts and are beyond the prescribed deadline of submission to the COA; and Augment the Accounting Unit with competent personnel to assist in accounting works to facilitate timely submission of reports as not to delay the audit of transactions and operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the BS; and The LCE should provide additional staff to the Municipal Accountant to assist in the preparation of the financial reports and supporting schedules/documents.

The municipal officials and employees tasked with procurement functions should strictly adhere with the mode of procurement indicated in the Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality, avoid splitting of contracts and minimize shopping and small value as the preferred modes of procurement, if public bidding is required.
The PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

Copy of any contract, letter order, job order or purchase order irrespective of amount, and all its supporting documents shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned within five (5) working days from issuance thereof to facilitate review and evaluation.

Each office to prepare a Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) that should include only those procurements which are crucial to the efficient discharge of each office’s function. The PPMP shall then be submitted to the procuring entity’s Budget Office for evaluation and if warranted, for inclusion in the procuring entity’s budget proposal for approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity. The BAC Secretariat will review and consolidate the PPMPs into the proposed APP of the Municipality.

All collectors to prepare the RAAF at the end of each month to facilitate review by the Municipal Treasurer as to initial balances, receipts, issuances and ending balances, on hand. The Municipal Treasurer should consolidate the reports of all the collectors in the CRAAF which should be submitted to the Auditor for review and verification.

Creation of policies and procedures on the payment for repairs and maintenance of property and equipment of the Municipality, be it regular, preventive, and corrective maintenance or emergency repairs, and observe the limitations prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-003; General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to properly evaluate the cost of repair and maintenance of the equipment owned by the Municipality, and the General Services Officer to maintain Waste Materials Report for the parts to be replaced; Concerned officers and employees to undergo proper procurement processes in accordance with RA No. 9184, for repairs and maintenance of property and equipment.

      Municipal Accountant.

        National Government Agencies in order to settle the obligations  of
