      •  Submit  Accomplishment  Report  or  Status  of  all   PPAs   being
        implemented by the  Municipal  Government  to  the  Office  of  the
        Auditor; and


      • Implement, as soon as possible, those  projects  whose  funds  were
        already transferred to the Municipal Government’s Bank Account  for
        more than one year and not fully executed  or  carried  out  as  of
        December 31, 2019 for the benefit of the residents/constituents  of
        the Municipality.


   2. Acting Municipal Accountant to analyze the breakdown of  the  accounts
      presented in the Trial Balance, gather relevant documents in order  to
      establish valid obligations, provide the necessary subsidiary  ledgers
      to support  the  balances  and  make  the  necessary  adjustments,  if
      warranted, to clean up the Due to NGAs Account.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct:


   1. All the concerned Municipal  Officials  and  Employees  to  cause  the
      immediate liquidation of cash advances in accordance  with  the  rules
      and regulations and observe the prescribed periods of liquidation  for
      all cash advances; and


   2. The Municipal Accountant to:

     a. Send within ten (10) days  before  the  expiration  of  the  30-day
        period specified under Section 14 of  EO  298  for  official  local
        travel a written reminder under signature of the head of agency  or
        his authorized representative, enjoining the official  or  employee
        concerned to liquidate his/her travel cash advance/s,  pursuant  to
        the rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advance;


     b. Refrain from granting additional cash advance to  any  official  or
        employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first
        settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; and


     c. Reconcile as soon as possible the difference  between  the  balance
        reported in the status reports and  the  amount  disclosed  in  the
        Notes to the Financial Statements and submit the updated report  to
        the Office of the Auditor.


   3. All  Accountable  Officers  with  unliquidated  Cash  Advances  as  of
      December 31, 2019 to liquidate the outstanding amount as of  June  30,
      2020. Audit suspensions/disallowances, if warranted,  will  be  issued
      for  those  cash  advances  that  are   not   liquidated   after   the
      aforementioned date.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:


   1. Compute the borrowing cost to be capitalized as at year-end  according
      to the Standard and recognize it as part of the cost of the qualifying
      asset by making a correcting entry; and


   2. Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements (a)  the  accounting
      policy adopted for borrowing costs; (b) the amount of borrowing  costs
      capitalized during the period; and (c) the capitalization rate used to
      determine the amount of borrowing costs  eligible  for  capitalization
      (when it was  necessary  to  apply  a  capitalization  rate  to  funds
      borrowed generally) as prescribed by Paragraph 40 of PPSAS 5.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE require the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

   1.  Maintain  subsidiary  ledgers  for  each  existing  stallholder   for
      monitoring and record-keeping purposes; and


   2. Make the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the amount  receivable
      from the market stallholders at year end.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the:


   1. Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to
      reflect the correct balances of  the  affected  expense  accounts  and
      henceforth, use appropriate accounts in recording transactions; and


   2. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that there is an appropriate budget
      for training and travelling  expenses  before  certifying  as  to  the
      existence of available appropriation pursuant to Section 4 (1)  of  PD
      No. 1445, which states that no money shall be paid out of  any  public
      treasury or depository except in pursuance of an appropriation law  or
      other specific statutory authority, in order  to  avoid  future  audit
      suspensions/disallowances.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. Human Resource and Management Officer:


      • Furnish every Office the updated schedule of leave credits earned of
        the Officials/Employees concerned as often  as  practicable.  Earned
        vacation and sick leave shall be presented separately. The data  can
        be used in drafting budget proposals relative to the monetization of
        leave credits  earned,  payment  of  terminal  leave  and  for  easy
        verification if the information presented therein were  accurate  or
        not; and


      • Ensure that Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules  on  Leave  were
        followed before certifying in the Application  for  Leave  on  every
        request for commutation of leave credits.


   2. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure  that  payments  of  leave  credits
      earned shall not exceed the approved appropriation in order  to  avoid
      audit suspensions/disallowances.


   3. The Acting Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entry
      in order to reflect the correct balances of accounts involved.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct:


   1. The Municipal Budget Officer to:


       • Submit as soon as possible the revised  computation  for  Personal
         Services (PS) limitation with complete details and information;


       •  Make  sure  the  existence  of  available  appropriation   before
         certifying in the Obligation  Request  in  order  to  avoid  audit
         suspension/disallowances.




       • See to it that appropriations for PS expenses will be  within  the
         limitation prescribed by Section 325 (a) of RA No. 7160


   2. The Acting Municipal  Accountant  to  ensure  that  disbursements  for
      incentives/bonuses/allowances are in accordance with  the  established
      rules and regulations before payment; and


   3. The Officials and Employees concerned to return, as soon as  possible,
      the incentives/bonuses/allowances received in excess  of  the  PS  Cap
      limitation    in    order    to    avoid     issuance     of     audit
      suspension/disallowances.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct:


   1. The Personnel Rewards and Recognition Committee to:


      • Establish an employee and incentive rewards  system  or  PRAISE  and
        shall  adhere  to  the  rules  and  regulations  prescribed  by  CSC
        Memorandum Circular No. 01, s. 2001;


      • Make sure that monetary  awards  shall  be  granted  only  when  the
        suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal
        effort of the nominee resulted in monetary savings which  shall  not
        exceed 20 percent of the savings generated; and




      • Submit the PRAISE and its amendments, if any, to the  Civil  Service
        Regional Office, copy furnished please the Office of the Audit  Team
        Leader.


   2. The Human Resource Management Officer to provide a  copy  of  the  job
      performance evaluation and assessment results showing if indeed  those
      awardees have suggestions, inventions,  superior  accomplishments  and
      other personal efforts which contribute to the efficiency, economy, or
      other improvement in government  operations,  or  other  extraordinary
      acts or services in the public interest.


   3. The Acting Municipal Accountant to reclassify the expenses as Personal
      Services based on its nature  and  make  sure,  henceforth,  that  the
      supporting documents for the Disbursement Vouchers are complete before
      payment;


   4. The Municipal Budget  Officer  to  ensure  that  there  is  indeed  an
      existence of available appropriation for a particular disbursement and
      the same are in accordance with  the  nature  of  the  account  before
      certifying the Obligation Request; and


   5.  The  Officials  and  Employees  concerned  to  return  the  Employees
      Incentive Allowance received as soon as possible to avoid issuance  of
      audit suspension/disallowance.

Recommendations:


   We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  require  the  Acting
   Municipal Treasurer to:


   1.  Execute  lease  contracts  with  market   stallholders   to   enhance
      enforceability of the ordinance and ensure  that  all  the  applicable
      terms and conditions were reflected therein; and


   2. Provide the Acting Municipal Accountant  data  e.g.,  list  of  market
      occupants, corresponding rental rate, the amount due from them  as  at
      December 31, 2019, and payments made thereafter.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE:

   1. Ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development  Fund  (DF)  and
      approve only those transactions that are expressly prescribed  by  law
      or public policy; and


   2.  Direct  the   persons   responsible   to   discontinue   payment   of
      administrative expenses and other non-allowable  charges  against  the
      20% DF to avoid audit disallowances.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned personnel to:


   1. Require the suppliers – SBT Store and CFCMPC  –  to  submit  the  BIR-
      registered Charge Invoices and Delivery Receipts,  and  properly  fill
      out the forms as prescribed by the NIRC. Present the original copy  of
      the Official Receipt issued by the CFCMC;


   2. Make sure that the  procurement  documents  e.g.,  canvass  forms  and
      Purchase Orders are properly and completely filled out  and  that  the
      suppliers are accredited. Section 53 of the Revised IRR for 2016 of RA
      No. 9184 states in part that Negotiated Procurement  is  a  method  of
      procurement of Goods, Infrastructure Projects and Consulting services,
      whereby the Procuring Entity negotiates a contract with a technically,
      legally and financially capable supplier, contractor or consultant;


   3. Verify the completeness of the supporting documents  and  validity  of
      the transactions before payment; and
   4. Henceforth, follow strictly the provisions prescribed by R.A. No. 9184
      on the appropriate mode of procurement during calamity to avoid future
      audit findings.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officers  and  employees
   to do the following:


   1.  Discontinue  the   practice   of   providing   commutable   RATA   to
      officers/employees entitled to reimbursable  RATA  pursuant  to  Local
      Budget Circular No. 103; and


   2. Provide the  complete  documentary  requirements  for  commutable  and
      reimbursable RATA claims as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Officials/Personnel to:


   1. Discontinue  the  practice  of  utilizing  the  fund  for  payment  of
      administrative expenses for the DepEd District  of  Carles  and  other
      expenses not related  to  or  included  in  the  list  of  prioritized
      expenses chargeable against  SEF,  in  order  to  avoid  future  audit
      suspensions/disallowances; and


   2. Submit a copy of the  Local  School  Board  Resolution  approving  the
      budget for priority education projects for easy  verification  of  the
      propriety, validity and legality of expenses charged under the Special
      Education Fund pursuant to Section 4(1) of PD No.  1445  which  states
      that no money shall be paid out of any public treasury in pursuance of
      an appropriation law or other specific statutory authority.

Recommendations:


   We recommended that the LCE direct the  responsible/concerned  department
   heads to do the following (based on  the  guidelines  prescribed  by  COA
   Circular No. 2018-002):


   1. Secure directly from the GSIS GIF, all insurances  or  bonds  covering
      properties, contracts, rights of action, and other insurable risks  of
      their respective offices;


   2. Prepare the Property Inventory Form (PIF)  listing  of  the  insurable
      properties  and  other  assets,   showing   their   latest   appraised
      values/valuation, appraised date, location, and other information;


   3. Extract from the Report on the Physical Count of PPE the data for  the
      PIF pertaining to the insurable assets and interest of the government;


   4. Cause the appraisal of the insurable properties and  other  assets  of
      their respective offices;


   5. Submit the consolidated PIF to the Office of the Auditor and the  GIF,
      GSIS, not later than April 30 of each year;


   6. Include in the annual appropriation the amount  of  premiums  for  the
      general insurance covering all insurable assets and other  assets  and
      ensure its payment to the GSIS; and


   7. Ensure centralized payment  of  insurance  premiums  to  avoid  double
      payment.

Recommendations:

   We recommended that the LCE:


   1. Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant  to  submit  immediately
      the Trial Balances and Reports with all the  supporting  documents  of
      the months that are already recorded in the books of accounts and  are
      beyond the prescribed deadline of submission to the Auditor; and


   2. Augment the Accounting Unit, if needed, with  competent  personnel  to
      assist in accounting works to facilitate timely submission of  reports
      and not  delay  the  audit  of  transactions  and  operations  of  the
      Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee exert more effort in conducting the physical count of all its properties, maintaining property cards thereon, and preparing the reports with sufficient and more accurate information.
The Supply Officer to prepare procedures on the conduct of physical inventory of PPE and present such to the Agency Head for approval;

The Inventory Committee, with the Supply Officer as one of the members, to develop a plan to include a timeline for the physical inventory, who will supervise the count, the personnel to act as counters and checkers, and the sequence of the counting. A set of written instructions shall be prepared to be observed by each person connected with the inventory-taking. For furniture and fixtures, equipment and the like, inventory tags shall be serially pre-numbered and prepared in duplicate with a perforation separating the top and bottom portions of the tag;

The Inventory Committee to physically count all existing PPE and provide complete description. The GSO only counted the PPE purchased in CYs 2017-2018. Secure other information such as person accountable, date of purchase, brand, model, serial no., engine no., etc. and determine if unserviceable or not;
observations.reason: The GSO only counted the PPE purchased in CYs 2017-2018.

observations.recommendation: Based on the information gathered in procedure no. 3, determine if accounting records and property records were prepared for these PPEs. If there were none, the Municipal Accountant and GSO shall create a PPELC and PC for each PPE as discussed above. PPE without accounting records shall be properly disclosed;
observations.reason: Not all of the PPE have Property Cards.

observations.recommendation: For the current purchases of PPE, the Municipal Accountant and GSO shall prepare the PPELC and PC, respectively and issue the corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to the accountable employee; Management to submit the Report on physical inventory as required by regulations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant maintains soft copy of PPE accounts per office and per category.

observations.recommendation: The Office of the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, Assessor and GSO to coordinate with each other and look for documents/records relative to the parcels of land recorded in the books of accounts for its proper recognition. After appraisal of the parcels of land, the result will be the basis for the Office of the Municipal Accountant in the recording of the same in the books of accounts; and The Office of the Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions of Section 63 of PD No. 1445 and Paragraphs 26 and 27 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17 on the proper measurement on the recognition of the parcels of land and make the necessary correcting/adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Not implemented (second part).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to: 1. Trace back the unreconciled balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, and make the necessary adjustment, where appropriate, to reflect the correct account balances; and 2. Reconcile the reciprocal accounts on a regular basis to correct the difference as early as possible and avoid the build-up of discrepancies over time.

OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant to make necessary actions to enforce collection of the funds due to the government; Concerned accountable officers to remit all collections on hand and the liquidating officer to deposit the same not later than the next banking day; and Immediately preceding and the current OIC-Municipal Treasurer to resolve the abnormal balances by reconciling their respective accounts, making necessary adjustments and notifying the OIC-Municipal Accountant accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued by the current Municipal Treasurer to the identified accountable officers with outstanding balance for deposit; however, there are still undeposited collections.

observations.recommendation: Acting Municipal Accountant to immediately revert back to cash and set-up the corresponding liability account/s of the stale checks identified using the guidelines prescribed under Item 4 b and c, Section 83 of the DOF-BLGF Local Operations Manual; Acting Municipal Accountant to verify with the AGDB of the Municipality the validity of the subject credit memo, and immediately effect any adjustments in the books of accounts, if warranted; Acting Municipal Accountant to work hand in hand with the OIC-Municipal Treasurer in tracing the records of the transactions relative to the P32,000.00 deposit credited by the AGDB of the Municipality to its bank account but not recorded by the latter; Municipal Treasurer to request for snapshot bank statements for every transaction with the AGDB of the Municipality to facilitate prompt reconciliation of book and bank balances; and Municipal Treasurer to verify with the AGDB the veracity of the deposits made amounting to P30,000.00 which were recorded in the books but not credited by the bank. If found void, the OIC-Municipal Treasurer shall charge the floating amount to the responsible Accountable Officer who declared such deposit/s.
observations.managementAction: Credit memo was not verified. The Municipality has not identified the Accountable Officer to whom the reconciling items pertain. Municipal Treasurer requested snapshots; Credit memo verification partially implemented.
observations.reason: Credit memo was not verified. The Municipality has not identified the Accountable Officer to whom the reconciling items pertain.

observations.recommendation: The LCE to direct: All the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees to cause the immediate liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the rules and regulations and observe the prescribed periods of liquidation for all cash advances; The OIC-Municipal Accountant to: Send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30-day period specified under Section 14 of EO 298 for official local travel a written reminder under signature of the head of agency or his authorized representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate his/her travel cash advance/s, pursuant to the rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advance; Refrain from granting additional cash advance to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; Submit to the Office of the Auditor the DVs and supporting documents of those cash advances that are not liquidated up to this date for issuance of audit suspensions/disallowance, if warranted; Reconcile as soon as possible the difference between the balance reported in the Status of Unliquidated Cash Advances and the amount disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements and submit the updated report to the Office of the Auditor; The LCE to augment the Treasury Unit with competent personnel to assist the ICO-MTO by designating new disbursing officers to even out the burden of responsibility and accountability and so as not to delay the flow of transactions and operations of the Municipality. Personnel hired through Job Order Contract shall not be granted cash advances as prescribed in Section 48 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: The number of transactions decreased but the practice still exists. Reconciliation of prior years’ CAs is on-going. The Office of the Treasurer had vacant plantilla but was overhauled. Only one Job Hire was assigned to the Office.
observations.reason: The number of transactions decreased but the practice still exists. Reconciliation of prior years’ CAs is on-going.

observations.recommendation: The LCE direct the responsible personnel to: Conduct feasibility study as to the availability of water source since it is the most vital of all the processes in the realization of the project; Impose liquidated damages against the erring Contractor in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR, if warranted; and Comply with the responsibilities of the Municipal Government, as required by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-143, particularly on the organization of the Water Sanitation (WATSAN) Council/Committee and Poor Communities with no Access to Safe Drinking Water; and strengthen its monitoring functions, among others.
observations.reason: Not implemented (impose liquidated damages).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the following: Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to formulate strategies in order to efficiently and effectively implement all the projects funded under the Municipality’s continuing appropriations and ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF in compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; MPDC to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the projects under the 20% DF, and if the same is in accordance with the approved development plan and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program as prescribed in Section 2.2 of the above JMC.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following: MDRRMO to submit monthly to the Office of the Auditor the Report on Utilization of the DRRMF; Office of the Municipal Accountant to include a discussion on the details of the unexpended balances of the MDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements as required in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002. MDRRM Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the records on hand and present the correct balance of the unutilized MDRRMF in the Report on the Utilization of DRRM Fund and Notes to the FS and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly the reports.
observations.managementAction: Submitted annual utilization for CY 2019 but no monthly report. Report on Utilization of DRRM Fund for CY 2019 was submitted last February 22, 2020 only.
observations.reason: Submitted annual utilization for CY 2019 but no monthly report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the following: MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the monthly Report on utilization of the DRRMF; MDRRM Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and Other Persons Responsible to draft a budgetary proposal for the appropriation of MOOE and capital outlay of the MDRRM Office and discontinue charging the same to the 5% MDRRM Fund; otherwise, transactions may be suspended/disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Report on Utilization of DRRM Fund for CY 2019 was submitted last February 22, 2020 only.

observations.recommendation: MDRRM Officer to revisit the LDRRMO’s mandate, and prepare the LDRRM Plan in accordance with the provisions cited in Item No. 2 above for it to properly carry out its objectives.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following: Direct the full implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003, together with the members of the SWM Board, in order to avoid administrative sanctions stated in Section 50 of the Act; Require the SWM Task Force to impose the fees, fines and penalties due to the LGU as provided in Section 47 and 49 of the same Act; and Eventually require the establishment of a sanitary landfill which is the ultimate requirement in ensuring the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality as prescribed by RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Sanitary landfill was not built due to lack of funds but segregation of waste has been practiced and shredder machines were procured. Creation of task force was already referred to the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to examine the books of accounts and pertinent records of the businesses once every tax period and comply with the manner of examination as prescribed by the Manual.

We recommended that the LCE require the immediate execution of lease contracts with its market occupants to enhance enforceability of its ordinance and maintenance of its database for record-keeping and monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: Drafts were made but LCE plans to increase charges.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the GAD Focal Person, members of the GAD Focal Point System and concerned personnel to: Promptly submit the required GAD-related reports within the prescribed period; Be mindful of the gender issue or gender mandate of the Municipality in formulating the GAD Plan and Budget; Carefully identify the activities which can truly address either the client-focused or organization-focused gender issue prior to its inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget; Be aware of the expenses that are considered to be Irregular, Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant, and Unconscionable, and avoid incorporating the said expenses to the GAD PB and eventually pay the same, so as to prevent future audit suspensions/disallowances; and Refund the rice allowances received in CY 2018 and previous years as there was no law authorizing the grant of such allowances. The same shall apply to other programs/projects/activities that were paid even though it did not adhere with any established rules, regulations, procedural guidelines, policies, principles or practices that have gained recognition in laws. Concerned personnel to use appropriate Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tools in the attribution or mainstreaming of the Municipality’s program/projects/activities (PPAs) in the GAD Plan and Budget such as design checklist (HGDG Boxes 9-15, 18-23), the funding facilities checklist (HGDG Box F1) or the generic checklist (HGDG Box 7a). For its Accomplishment Report, the Municipality can use the Project Implementation and Management, and Monitoring and Evaluation (PIMME) checklist (HGDG Boxes 16 and 17) or the Facility Implementation, Management, and Monitoring and Evaluation (FIMME) checklist (HGDG Box F2).
observations.managementAction: The LGU has created Executive Order No. 48, Series of 2018, An Order Reconstituting the Gender and Development Focal Point System of the Municipal Government of Carles, Province of Iloilo.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not use HGDG tools; some requirements not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the following: Creation of an Appraisal Committee composed of the Municipal Assessor, Engineer, and Treasurer to facilitate the gathering of necessary data and information regarding the acquisition of lots and to appraise the value of the parcels of land owned by the Municipality; Designation of focal person responsible for assessing the costs to be incurred in titling of lots and the inclusion of costs related thereto in the Municipality’s budget; and GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and Real Property Ledger Cards for the parcels of land in compliance with Sections 119 and 120 under Chapter 7 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

Concerned officials and employees to adhere strictly and comply with the requirements of Section 5.14 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and as prescribed in Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances; Office of the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents before payment; HRMO to be thorough in monitoring the leave credit balances and preparing the certification of leave balances and other documents needed in payment of related claims.
observations.managementAction: Strict compliance partially implemented. The Municipal Accountant to check completeness before payment was partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: The persons responsible to submit to the Office of the Auditor the following: List of positions in the plantilla with the corresponding job description/actual duties that describe the basis for justifying that such positions are exposed to occupational risks and assigned to places stated in Item 16.1.1 of the abovementioned IRR; Written procedure in identifying the personnel who are entitled to hazard pay as prescribed under item 16.1.2.2 of the IRR. If none was prepared, provisions in Items 16.1.2.1 and 16.1.2.2 of the IRR shall be complied. Strict compliance of the above-cited provisions by providing complete supporting documents for future claims to establish its validity and in order to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances; The Municipal Accountant to check the accuracy of the claim and completeness of the required supporting documents before payment; The persons concerned, who have availed of the hazard pay, to submit the duly approved DTRs, Travel Reports and Individual Accomplishment Reports to the Office of the Auditor for further evaluation of the claim. Otherwise, audit suspensions/disallowances may be issued, if warranted; and The persons concerned to refund the excess claim of hazard pay, if any.
observations.managementAction: Strict compliance of some provisions partially implemented. Some supporting documents still not submitted.
observations.reason: Some requirements not implemented; persons concerned did not submit DTRs, Travel Reports and Individual Accomplishment Reports.

observations.recommendation: The LCE strictly monitor the fuel consumption of the Municipality and direct the responsible officers and employees to: Abide with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR by employing proper procurement process and accomplishing the related documentary requirements; Comply with the requirements set in COA Circular No. 77-61 by: Supporting fuel disbursements with the following: Serially numbered and duly approved Driver’s Trip Tickets; Requisition and issue Voucher or equivalent; and Preparing the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation and submit the said report to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and its officers and employees to be aware of the rules and regulations governing the claim for travel expenses and strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular Nos. 96-004 and 2012-001 to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances. We also recommended that the Municipality consider procurement of the Municipality’s airline tickets thru the DBM-PS by registering with the PS-PhilGEPS’ GFA, if feasible.
observations.reason: Delays usually caused by employees with incomplete liquidation papers.

observations.recommendation: The LCE direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit the Trial Balances and Reports with all the supporting documents within the prescribed deadline of submission to the Auditor; and Augment the Accounting Unit, if needed, with competent personnel to assist in accounting works to facilitate timely submission of reports and not delay the audit of transactions and operations of the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reports not promptly submitted. Two additional JOs were assigned to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; however, the manpower is not enough to complete the preparation of both Municipal and barangay transactions.
observations.reason: Monthly reports not promptly submitted; manpower insufficient.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2018 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices and the information gathered therein shall be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor as soon as possible. We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Government, shall within sixty (60) days from its receipt of the Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government submitted thru e-mail the CY 2018 AAPSI (unofficial – without Mayor’s signature) last March 10, 2019, and subsequently submitted official copy last May 28, 2020. The Municipal Government will do its best to consolidate and submit the AAPSI within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: The OIC-Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustments addressing the improper accounting entries made in the recording of installed and constructed assets; and The concerned department heads to attach the required supporting documents including bidding documents to validate the qualifications of the bidders.

The LCE to direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to: Demand from the collectors the prompt turnover of collections at the close of each business day; Request the collectors assigned in the field to remit to the Designated Liquidating Officer the collections at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach P5,000.00; Remind the Liquidating Officer to: Make sure that the Accountable Officers observe issuance of pre-numbered official receipts in strict numerical sequence and check that cancelled Accountable Forms are attached in the Report of Collections before submission to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and eventually to the Office of the Auditor for audit; Double check the correctness of the collections being remitted to avoid under/over remittance and sign the Reports accordingly; Deposit intact all collections turned over by the collectors with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.
observations.managementAction: Demand from the collectors the prompt turnover of collections; Request the collectors to remit at least once a week; Remind the Liquidating Officer to adhere to procedures; Deposit intact all collections daily.
observations.reason: Delays still occur; CRAAF were not promptly submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: The LCE to direct the Concerned Department Heads to include the projects, under the ADM Program, in the APP of the ensuing year considering that the projects are still ongoing; The persons responsible to re-assess the project schedule/timeline and the actual implementation of the project and impose liquidated damages, if warranted; The persons responsible to attach the complete bidding documents to facilitate the timely audit of disbursements; The Engineering Department to attach related pictures before, during and after the construction, detailed drawings/plans and specifications and other necessary documents. The said documents will be used by the Audit Team in conducting inspection of the implemented projects.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. The Municipality submitted the status of infrastructure projects only and did not contain the projects that are to be implemented in the current year. Consolidation and progress monitoring were not carried out by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: The LCE to: Inform the Office of the SA and ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, and direct the Concerned Department Heads to include in the Report of GPPAs, all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; Concerned Department Heads to maintain as current as possible the Project Status as mentioned in Section 3.2 of the Circular; and Assignment of a focal person that will consolidate/update the report and monitor the progress of the PPAs every reporting period and submit promptly to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. The Municipality submitted the status of infrastructure projects only and did not contain the projects that are to be implemented in the current year. Oftentimes, reports were submitted beyond the deadline. Only the Engineering Department in-charge of infrastructure projects submitted the report. Consolidation and progress monitoring were not carried out by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: The LCE to: Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the reports and supporting documents of the months that are already recorded in the books of accounts and are beyond the prescribed deadline of submission to the COA; and Augment the Accounting Unit with competent personnel to assist in accounting works to facilitate timely submission of reports and not delay the audit of transactions and operations of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Delays are usually caused by employees with incomplete liquidation papers. Two additional JOs were assigned to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; however, the manpower is not enough to complete the preparation of both Municipal and barangay transactions.

observations.recommendation: BAC to disqualify the CAMEA-MPC and other suppliers that could qualify as having conflict of interest stated in Volume 1 of the Generic Procurement Manuals of the GPPB and as expressed in the GPPB NPM 142-2014; BAC to observe the proper process of procurement as presented in RA No. 9184 by carefully evaluating each Purchase Request and recommending the appropriate mode suited to the items in the PR; Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents before payment and require the persons responsible to attach documents that are free of alterations/inconsistencies to avoid doubt on the authenticity and validity of the disbursements.

The Municipal Accountant shall always ensure the approval of proper officials and that complete documentation are attached to the disbursement vouchers to establish legality and validity of the claims. Adherence to documentary requirements prescribed under existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations should be strictly observed.

The LCE to direct the OIC Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and stop the practice of granting cash advances prior to the liquidation/settlement of previous cash advances.

The Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the BS. The LCE should provide additional staff to the Municipal Accountant to assist in the preparation of the financial reports and supporting schedules/documents.
observations.managementAction: Delayed submission due to late receipt of bank statements. Two additional JOs were assigned to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; however, the manpower is not enough to complete the preparation of both Municipal and barangay transactions.
observations.reason: Delayed submission due to late receipt of bank statements; manpower is insufficient.

observations.recommendation: The municipal officials and employees tasked with procurement functions should strictly adhere with the mode of procurement indicated in the Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality, avoid splitting of contracts and minimize shopping and small value as the preferred modes of procurement, if public bidding is required.

The Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to remit to the concerned government agencies all moneys withheld from all sources within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445, Section 4.114-2 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005, RA Nos. 8291, 9679 and 7875 and their IRRs, otherwise, surcharges, interests and penalties on late remittances would be his/her personal liability.
observations.managementAction: Total Due to the BIR, GSIS, PHIC and HDMF amounted to P1,803,363.48. Due to NGAs at end of year is for remittance within ten days from end of the month to which the withheld amount pertains.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality shall fund projects out of the 20% DF which contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
The PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.
observations.managementAction: There is an internal arrangement between the employees and the Municipal Accountant that the remaining balance due to the government shall be deducted from the payroll by the employees on installment basis or offset against claims e.g., bonuses.

observations.recommendation: The concerned official shall refund the excess travel claim and submit the required documents as shown in the detailed schedule. The Municipal Accountant to review the travel claims of local officials or employees and see to it that complete and correct supporting documents are attached before signing the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant to review the travel claims of local officials or employees and see to it that complete and correct supporting documents are attached before signing the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality to adopt the mode of procurement embodied in its Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for its programs, projects and activities. When any of the alternative modes of procurement will be resorted and recommended to the head of the procuring entity, the BAC should ensure that the conditions enumerated therein exist. The concerned personnel to ensure that the supporting documents are complete and correctly filled up in compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. There was no action taken by the Management to implement the audit recommendations. The concerned personnel to ensure that the supporting documents are complete and correctly filled up in compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 was partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: Copy of any contract, letter order, job order or purchase order irrespective of amount, and all its supporting documents shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned within five (5) working days from issuance thereof to facilitate review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. Not all POs and other relevant documents were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Each office to prepare a Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) that should include only those procurements which are crucial to the efficient discharge of each office’s function. The PPMP shall then be submitted to the procuring entity’s Budget Office for evaluation and if warranted, for inclusion in the procuring entity’s budget proposal for approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity. The BAC Secretariat will review and consolidate the PPMPs into the proposed APP of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. Not all of the items for procurement were included in the APP.

observations.recommendation: All collectors to prepare the RAAF at the end of each month to facilitate review by the Municipal Treasurer as to initial balances, receipts, issuances and ending balances, on hand. The Municipal Treasurer should consolidate the reports of all the collectors in the CRAAF which should be submitted to the Auditor for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented. CRAAF were not promptly submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended  that  the  Inventory  Committee  exert  more  effort  in
   conducting the physical count of all its properties, maintaining property
   cards thereon,  and  preparing  the  reports  with  sufficient  and  more
   accurate information.

We recommended the following:


   1. The Office of the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, Assessor and GSO to
      coordinate with each other and look for documents/records relative  to
      the parcels of land recorded in the books of accounts for  its  proper
      recognition. After appraisal of the parcels of land, the  result  will
      be the basis for  the  Office  of  the  Municipal  Accountant  in  the
      recording of the same in the books of accounts; and



   2. The Office of the Municipal Accountant to observe  the  provisions  of
      Section 63 of PD No. 1445 and Paragraphs 26 and 27 of  the  Philippine
      Public Sector Accounting Standards 17 on the proper measurement on the
      recognition  of  the  parcels  of  land   and   make   the   necessary
      correcting/adjusting entries.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:


   1. Trace back the unreconciled balances of Due from Other Funds  and  Due
      to Other Funds accounts, and  make  the  necessary  adjustment,  where
      appropriate, to reflect the correct account balances; and


   2. Reconcile the reciprocal accounts on a regular basis  to  correct  the
      difference  as  early  as  possible  and   avoid   the   build-up   of
      discrepancies over time.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. OIC-Municipal  Treasurer  and  Acting  Municipal  Accountant  to  make
      necessary actions to enforce  collection  of  the  funds  due  to  the
      government;

   2. Concerned accountable officers to remit all collections  on  hand  and
      the liquidating officer to deposit the same not later  than  the  next
      banking day; and

   3. Immediately preceding  and  the  current  OIC-Municipal  Treasurer  to
      resolve  the  abnormal  balances  by  reconciling   their   respective
      accounts, making necessary adjustments and notifying the OIC-Municipal
      Accountant accordingly.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. Acting Municipal Accountant to immediately revert back to cash and set-
      up  the  corresponding  liability  account/s  of  the   stale   checks
      identified using the guidelines prescribed  under  Item  4  b  and  c,
      Section 83 (b) of the DOF-BLGF Local Operations Manual;


   2.  Acting  Municipal  Accountant  to  verify  with  the  AGDB   of   the
      Municipality the validity of the subject credit memo, and  immediately
      effect any adjustments in the books of accounts, if warranted;



   3. Acting Municipal Accountant  to  work  hand  in  hand  with  the  OIC-
      Municipal  Treasurer  in  tracing  the  records  of  the  transactions
      relative to the  P32,000.00  deposit  credited  by  the  AGDB  of  the
      Municipality to its bank account but not recorded by the latter;



   4. Municipal Treasurer to request for snapshot bank statements for  every
      transaction with the AGDB of the  Municipality  to  facilitate  prompt
      reconciliation of book and bank balances; and



   5. Municipal Treasurer to verify  with  the  AGDB  the  veracity  of  the
      deposits made amounting to P30,000.00 which were recorded in the books
      but not credited by the bank.


      If found void, the OIC-Municipal Treasurer shall charge  the  floating
      amount to  the  responsible  Accountable  Officer  who  declared  such
      deposit/s.

We recommended that the LCE direct:


   1. All the concerned Municipal  Officials  and  Employees  to  cause  the
      immediate liquidation of cash advances in accordance  with  the  rules
      and regulations and observe the prescribed periods of liquidation  for
      all cash advances; and


   2. The Municipal Accountant to:


     1. Send within ten (10) days  before  the  expiration  of  the  30-day
        period specified under Section 14 of  EO  298  for  official  local
        travel a written reminder under signature of the head of agency  or
        his authorized representative, enjoining the official  or  employee
        concerned to liquidate his/her travel cash advance/s,  pursuant  to
        the rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advance;


     2. Refrain from granting additional cash advance to  any  official  or
        employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first
        settled or a proper accounting thereof is made;



     3. Submit to  the  Office  of  the  Auditor  the  DVs  and  supporting
        documents of those cash advances that are not liquidated up to this
        date for issuance of audit suspensions/disallowance, if  warranted;
        and


     4. Reconcile as soon as possible the difference  between  the  balance
        reported in the Status of Unliquidated Cash Advances and the amount
        disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements and  submit  the
        updated report to the Office of the Auditor.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  (LCE)   direct   the
   responsible personnel to:

   1. Conduct feasibility study as to the availability of water source since
      it is the most vital of all the processes in the  realization  of  the
      project;




   2. Impose liquidated damages against the erring Contractor in  accordance
      with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR, if warranted; and


   3. Comply with the  responsibilities  of  the  Municipal  Government,  as
      required by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-143, particularly on the
      organization of the Water Sanitation  (WATSAN)  Council/Committee  and
      Poor Communities with no Access to Safe Drinking Water; and strengthen
      its monitoring functions, among others.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1.  Municipal  Engineer  and  the  Municipal  Planning  and   Development
      Coordinator (MPDC) to formulate strategies in order to efficiently and
      effectively implement all the projects funded under the Municipality’s
      continuing appropriations and ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF
      in compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and


   2. MPDC to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the projects  under
      the 20% DF, and if  the  same  is  in  accordance  with  the  approved
      development plan and directly supportive of the Philippine Development
      Plan and Public Investment Program as prescribed in Section 2.2 of the
      above JMC.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following꞉


   1. MDRRMO to submit monthly to the Office of the Auditor  the  Report  on
      Utilization of the DRRMF; and


   2. Office of the Municipal Accountant to  include  a  discussion  on  the
      details of the unexpended balances of the MDRRMF in the Notes  to  the
      Financial Statements as required in Annex D of COA Circular No.  2012-
      002.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. MDRRM Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and Other Persons  Responsible
      to draft a budgetary  proposal  for  the  appropriation  of  MOOE  and
      capital outlay of the MDRRM Office and avoid charging the same to  the
      5% MDRRM Fund; otherwise, transactions could  be  suspended/disallowed
      in audit; and



   2. MDRRM Officer to revisit the LDRRMO’s mandate, and prepare  the  LDRRM
      Plan in accordance with the provisions cited in Item No. 2  above  for
      it to properly carry out its objectives.

We recommended that the LCE:

   1. Direct the full implementation of  the  provisions  of  RA  No.  9003,
      together with the  members  of  the  SWM  Board,  in  order  to  avoid
      administrative sanctions stated in Section 50 of the Act;



   2. Require the SWM Task Force to impose the fees, fines and penalties due
      to the LGU as provided in Section 47 and 49 of the same Act; and

   3. Eventually require the establishment of a sanitary landfill  which  is
      the ultimate requirement in ensuring  the  protection  of  the  public
      health and environment of the Municipality as  prescribed  by  RA  No.
      9003.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Office of the Municipal  Treasurer
   to examine the books of accounts and pertinent records of the  businesses
   once every tax period and  comply  with  the  manner  of  examination  as
   prescribed by the Manual.

We recommended that the LCE require  the  immediate  execution  of  lease
   contracts with its market occupants  to  enhance  enforceability  of  its
   ordinance  and  maintenance  of  its  database  for  record-keeping   and
   monitoring purposes.

We recommended that the LCE direct the GAD Focal Person, members  of  the
   GAD Focal Point System and concerned personnel to:


   1. Promptly submit the required GAD-related reports within the prescribed
      period;


   2. Be mindful of the gender issue or gender mandate of  the  Municipality
      in formulating the GAD Plan and Budget;


   3. Carefully identify the activities which can truly address  either  the
      client-focused or  organization-focused  gender  issue  prior  to  its
      inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget;


   4. Be aware  of  the  expenses  that  are  considered  to  be  Irregular,
      Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant,  and  Unconscionable,  and  avoid
      incorporating the said expenses to the GAD PB and eventually  pay  the
      same, so as to prevent future audit suspensions/disallowances; and


   5. Refund the rice allowances received in CY 2018 and previous  years  as
      there was no law authorizing the grant of such  allowances.  The  same
      shall apply to other programs/projects/activities that were paid  even
      though it did not adhere  with  any  established  rules,  regulations,
      procedural guidelines, policies, principles  or  practices  that  have
      gained recognition in laws.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. Creation of an Appraisal Committee composed of the Municipal Assessor,
      Engineer, and Treasurer to facilitate the gathering of necessary  data
      and information regarding the acquisition of lots and to appraise  the
      value of the parcels of land owned by the Municipality;



   2. Designation of focal person responsible for assessing the costs to  be
      incurred in titling of lots and the inclusion of costs related thereto
      in the Municipality’s budget; and



   3. GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards  and  Real
      Property Ledger Cards for the  parcels  of  land  in  compliance  with
      Sections 119 and 120  under  Chapter  7  of  the  Manual  on  the  New
      Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. Concerned officials and employees to adhere strictly and  comply  with
      the requirements of Section 5.14 of COA Circular No. 2012-001  and  as
      prescribed in Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on  Leave,  Rule
      XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of  Executive  Order  No.
      292 to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances;


   2. Office of the  Municipal  Accountant  to  check  the  completeness  of
      supporting documents before payment; and


   3. HRMO to be thorough  in  monitoring  the  leave  credit  balances  and
      preparing the certification of  leave  balances  and  other  documents
      needed in payment of related claims.

We recommended that the LCE direct:

   1. The persons responsible to submit to the Office  of  the  Auditor  the
      following:



      • List of positions in  the  plantilla  with  the  corresponding  job
        description/actual duties that describe the  basis  for  justifying
        that such positions are exposed to occupational risks and  assigned
        to places stated in Item 16.1.1 of the abovementioned IRR; and



      • Written procedure in identifying the personnel who are entitled  to
        hazard pay as prescribed under item 16.1.2.2 of the IRR.


        If none was prepared, provisions in Items 16.1.2.1 and 16.1.2.2  of
        the IRR shall be complied.



   2. Strict compliance of the above-cited provisions by providing  complete
      supporting documents for future claims to establish its  validity  and
      in order to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances;


   3. The Municipal Accountant to  check  the  accuracy  of  the  claim  and
      completeness of the required supporting documents before payment;


   4. The persons concerned, who have availed of the hazard pay,  to  submit
      the duly approved DTRs, Travel Reports and  Individual  Accomplishment
      Reports to the Office of the Auditor for  further  evaluation  of  the
      claim. Otherwise, audit suspensions/disallowances may  be  issued,  if
      warranted; and


   5. The persons concerned to refund the excess claim  of  hazard  pay,  if
      any.

We recommended that the LCE strictly monitor the fuel consumption of  the
   Municipality and direct the responsible officers and employees to:


   1. Abide with the provisions of RA No. 9184  and  its  IRR  by  employing
      proper procurement process and accomplishing the  related  documentary
      requirements; and


   2. Comply with the requirements set in COA Circular No. 77-61 by:

     a. Supporting fuel disbursements with the following:
          i. Serially numbered and duly approved Driver’s Trip Tickets
         ii. Requisition and issue Voucher or equivalent; and


     b. Preparing the Monthly Report  of  Fuel  Consumption  of  government
        motor transportation and submit the said report to  the  Office  of
        the Auditor.

We recommended that the LCE  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  its
   officers and employees to be aware of the rules and regulations governing
   the claim for travel expenses and strictly comply with  the  requirements
   of   COA   Circular   Nos.   96-004   and   2012-001   to   avoid   audit
   suspensions/disallowances.


   We also recommended that the Municipality  consider  procurement  of  the
   Municipality’s airline tickets thru the DBM-PS by registering with the PS-
   PhilGEPS’ GFA, if feasible.

We recommended that the LCE direct the following:


   1. Creation of policies and procedures on the  payment  for  repairs  and
      maintenance of property and  equipment  of  the  Municipality,  be  it
      regular, preventive, and corrective maintenance or emergency  repairs,
      and observe the limitations prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-003;


   2. General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to  properly  evaluate
      the cost of repair and maintenance  of  the  equipment  owned  by  the
      Municipality, and the  General  Services  Officer  to  maintain  Waste
      Materials Report for the parts to be replaced; and


   3.  Concerned  officers  and  employees  to  undergo  proper  procurement
      processes in accordance with RA No. 9184, for repairs and  maintenance
      of property and equipment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive꞉


   1. Direct the Office of the Municipal  Accountant  to  submit  the  Trial
      Balances and Reports with all  the  supporting  documents  within  the
      prescribed deadline of submission to the Auditor; and


   2. Augment the Accounting Unit, if needed, with  competent  personnel  to
      assist in accounting works to facilitate timely submission of  reports
      and not  delay  the  audit  of  transactions  and  operations  of  the
      Municipal Government.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal  Government,  shall  within
   sixty (60) days from its receipt of the Annual Audit Report submit to the
   COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic  document,  a  status
   report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.
The GSO to prepare procedures on the conduct of physical inventory of PPE and present such to the Agency Head for approval;

The Inventory Committee, with the GSO as one of the members, to develop a plan to include a timeline for the physical inventory, who will supervise the count, the personnel to act as counters and checkers, and the sequence of the counting. A set of written instructions shall be prepared to be observed by each person connected with the inventory-taking. For furniture and fixtures, equipment and the like, inventory tags shall be serially pre-numbered and prepared in duplicate with a perforation separating the top and bottom portions of the tag;
Management to submit the Report on physical inventory as required by regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct proper coordination among personnel in charge of recording transactions which involve reciprocal accounts, determine the cause of the differences and subsequently reconcile such accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts on a regular basis to correct the difference as early as possible and avoid the buildup of discrepancies over time.

Devise a comprehensive SWM plan by providing time table for the implementation of the solid waste program that shall ensure long-term management of solid waste, as well as integrate the various solid waste management plans and strategies of the barangays and have it approved by the Commission to avail of additional funding for implementation; and
MDRRMO to only charge expenses in the 5% MDRRMF that are related/connected to the implementation of its PPAs;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to remit to the concerned government agencies all moneys withheld from all sources within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445, Section 4.114-2 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005, RA Nos. 8291, 9679 and 7875 and their IRRs, otherwise, surcharges, interests and penalties on late remittances would be his/her personal liability.
Assign a focal person that will consolidate/update the report and monitor the progress of the PPAs every reporting period.
We recommended that the concerned official shall refund the excess travel claim and submit the required documents as shown in the detailed schedule.

We recommended that the Municipality adopt the mode of procurement embodied in its Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for its programs, projects and activities.  When any of the alternative modes of procurement will be resorted and recommended to the head of the procuring entity, the BAC should ensure that the conditions enumerated therein exist.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustments addressing the improper accounting entries made in the recording of installed and constructed assets; and
The concerned department heads to attach the required supporting documents including bidding documents to validate the qualifications of the bidders.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
The Officials/Personnel concerned to:
Ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with the rules and regulations prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002;
Practice fiscal responsibility in all the transactions of the Municipality and make sure that cash advances for local travels shall be liquidated within 30 days after return to their official station and refund of unused cash advance should be immediately and simultaneously made with the liquidation; and
Immediately liquidate/settle long outstanding Cash Advances under their names.
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
Retain a copy of the disbursement voucher and itinerary of travel, keep an index of the cash advances made by each official/employee, monitor the liquidation of the same pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-004 and refrain from allowing additional cash advances to the same employee unless the previous one has been liquidated and duly accounted for in the books as prescribed in Section 89 of PD No. 1445; and
Enforce the provisions specified in the demand letters to all the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the:
Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to formulate strategies in order to efficiently and effectively implement all the projects funded under the Municipality’s continuing appropriations and ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF of CY 2017 in compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and
MPDC to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the projects under the 20% DF and if the same is in accordance with the approved development plan and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program as prescribed in Section 2.2 of the above JMC.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the full implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003, together with the members of the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Board;
Require the SWM Task Force to impose the fees, fines and penalties due to the Municipality as provided in Sections 47 and 49 of RA No. 9003; and
Eventually establish a sanitary landfill which is the ultimate requirement in ensuring the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality as prescribed by RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRM Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and Other Persons Responsible to draft a budgetary proposal for the appropriation of mooe and capital outlay of the MDRRM Office in order not to charge the same to the 5% MDRRM Fund and to avoid future notice of suspension/disallowance.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
MDRRM Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the records on hand and present the correct balance of the unutilized MDRRM Fund in the Report on the Utilization of DRRM Fund and Notes to the FS and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and
OIC-Municipal Accountant to include a discussion on the details of the unexpended balances of the MDRRM Fund in the Notes to the FS as required in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the LCE shall henceforth, inform the Office of the SA and ATL within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, and direct the:
Concerned Department Heads to include in the Report of GPPAs, all programs and activities with ongoing implementation and those to be implemented during the year, and diligently adhere to the provisions of Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004;
Concerned Department Heads to maintain as current as possible the Project Status as mentioned in Section 3.2 of the Circular; and

When any of the alternative modes of procurement will be resorted and recommended to the head of the procuring entity, the BAC should ensure that the conditions enumerated therein exist. We also recommended that the concerned personnel ensure that the supporting documents are complete and correctly filled up in compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that copy of any contract, letter order, job order or purchase order irrespective of amount, and all its supporting documents shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned within five (5) working days from issuance thereof to facilitate review and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipality should provide at least 5% of the total budget for GAD related projects. We also recommended that the Municipality should fully utilize the GAD Budget in order to recognize the fundamental equality of women and men and ensure gender responsive governance.

We recommended that the Municipality shall notify the public of the programs/projects/activities undertaken in their area for the promotion of good governance through transparency and accountability and as provided in COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall always ensure the approval of proper officials and that complete documentation are attached to the disbursement vouchers to establish legality and validity of the claims.  Adherence to documentary requirements prescribed under existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations should be strictly observed.

We recommended that each office prepare a Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) that should include only those procurements which are crucial to the efficient discharge of each office’s function.  The PPMP shall then be submitted to the procuring entity’s Budget Office for evaluation and if warranted, for inclusion in the procuring entity’s budget proposal for approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity. The BAC Secretariat will review and consolidate the PPMPs into the proposed APP of the Municipality.
